result.stderr:
FormatConverter 2.1.0+4046217f258c7d0cc6d4ed6e0d1b06a46ea07f71
Copyright (C) 2026 FormatConverter

  -i FILE, --input=FILE            Required. Path to input file (or '-' for
                                   stdin)

  --input-format=FORMAT            Required. Format of the input data (json,
                                   xml, yaml, messagepack, cbor, protobuf, bxml,
                                   toml)

  --output-format=FORMAT           Required. Desired format for the output data
                                   (json, xml, yaml, messagepack, cbor,
                                   protobuf, bxml, toml)

  -o FILE, --output=FILE           Output file path (if not specified, will be
                                   auto-generated based on input file name)

  -f, --force                      Overwrite output file if it already exists

  -v LEVEL, --verbosity=LEVEL      Set verbosity level (None, Error, Warning,
                                   Info, Debug, Trace)

  --version                        Show version info and exit

  -l, --list-formats               List all supported formats and exit

  --encoding                       (Default: utf-8) Input/output file encoding
                                   (utf-8, utf-16, ascii, etc.)

  --indent=SIZE                    Indentation size for formatted output (JSON,
                                   XML, YAML). Use 0 for tabs

  --minify                         Minify output by removing unnecessary
                                   whitespace

  --pretty                         Pretty print output with proper formatting
                                   and indentation

  --no-metadata                    Strip metadata, comments, and processing
                                   instructions from output

  --sort-keys                      Sort object keys alphabetically in output

  --json-escape-unicode            Escape non-ASCII characters in JSON output

  --json-allow-trailing-commas     Allow trailing commas in JSON input
                                   (non-standard but commonly used)

  --json-strict-property-names     (Default: true) Require quoted property names
                                   in JSON input (default: true, set to false to
                                   allow unquoted names)

  --json-allow-single-quotes       Allow single quotes in JSON input
                                   (non-standard, only works with direct string
                                   parsing)

  --xml-root=NAME                  Custom root element name for XML output

  --xml-namespace=URI              XML namespace URI for output

  --xml-namespace-prefix=PREFIX    XML namespace prefix

  --xml-declaration                Include XML declaration (<?xml
                                   version='1.0'?>)

  --xml-standalone                 Set standalone='yes' in XML declaration

  --xml-cdata                      Wrap text content in CDATA sections when
                                   necessary

  --xml-attributes                 Convert simple properties to XML attributes
                                   instead of elements

  --yaml-preserve-leading-zeros    (Default: true) Preserve leading zeros in
                                   numeric strings (e.g., '01234' stays as
                                   string)

  --yaml-flow-style                Use flow style (inline) for YAML output

  --yaml-explicit-start            Include explicit document start marker (---)
                                   in YAML

  --yaml-explicit-end              Include explicit document end marker (...) in
                                   YAML

  --yaml-quote-strings             Force quoting of all string values in YAML

  --yaml-canonical                 Use canonical YAML format

  --yaml-allow-duplicate-keys      Allow duplicate keys in YAML

  --toml-array-of-tables           (Default: true) Convert object arrays to TOML
                                   array of tables format ([[table]])

  --toml-multiline-strings         (Default: false) Use multiline string format
                                   ("""...""") for long strings

  --toml-strict-types              (Default: false) Enforce strict type
                                   conversion (fail if types cannot be
                                   represented in TOML)

  --toml-array-wrapper-key         Wrap root array; uses 'item' if not specified

  --msgpack-contractless           (Default: true) Use contractless resolver for
                                   MessagePack (allows dynamic types)

  --msgpack-old-spec               Use old MessagePack specification format

  --cbor-indefinite-length         (Default: true) Allow indefinite-length
                                   arrays and maps in CBOR

  --cbor-multiple-content          Allow multiple top-level CBOR items in
                                   sequence

  --cbor-canonical                 Use canonical CBOR encoding (sorted keys,
                                   minimal encoding)

  --cbor-tags                      Preserve CBOR semantic tags in conversion

  --cbor-datetime-tag              Use CBOR datetime tags (tag 0 or 1) for date
                                   values

  --cbor-bignum                    Use CBOR bignum tags (tag 2/3) for very large
                                   numbers

  --bxml-endian                    (Default: littleendian) Use 'littleendian'
                                   for Intel/x86 systems (default) or
                                   'bigendian' for network order and some RISC
                                   architectures.

  --bxml-compressArrays            (Default: true) Enables compression for
                                   homogeneous arrays to reduce output size

  --compress=TYPE                  Compress output using specified algorithm
                                   (gzip, deflate, brotli, lz4)

  --strict                         Enable strict validation mode (fail on
                                   warnings)

  --ignore-errors                  Continue processing even if non-critical
                                   errors occur

  --streaming                      Use streaming parser for large files (reduces
                                   memory usage)

  --chunk-size                     (Default: 100) Number of items per chunk when
                                   streaming

  --number-format=FORMAT           Number format for output (decimal,
                                   hexadecimal, scientific, binary, raw)

  --date-format=FORMAT             Date format string (e.g., 'yyyy-MM-dd',
                                   'ISO8601', 'unix')

  --timezone=TZ                    Target timezone for date conversion (e.g.,
                                   'UTC', 'America/New_York')

  --array-wrap                     Wrap single items in arrays when converting

  --flatten-arrays                 Flatten nested arrays in output

  --max-depth=DEPTH                Maximum nesting depth for objects/arrays

  --help                           Display this help screen.

  --version                        Display version information.
